info.title: sqlbook API
info.description:
API reference for the current sqlbook app surface.

The documented scope is still growing, but the API already covers the workspace, team-management, datasource, query, visualization, and visualization-theme contracts that are currently exposed through the product, chat runtime, and related app flows.

This API is session-authenticated, strictly workspace-scoped, and server-authoritative: every request is checked against the current user, the current workspace, and the same role/scope rules used by the product UI and chat runtime.

For LLM and tool consumers, treat each operation as an executable contract. Do not infer permissions from docs alone: always rely on response status plus the operation-specific `code` or `error_code`. Mutating actions may still be blocked by role or workspace scope even when the payload is structurally valid.

tags.name: Workspace
tags.description: Operations that modify the current workspace itself. These stay in the active workspace only and never cross workspace boundaries.

tags.name: Chat Threads
tags.description: Operations for renaming the current user's private chat threads inside a workspace. Thread actions are still workspace-scoped and may only affect the actor's own threads.

tags.name: Members
tags.description: Operations for listing, inviting, updating, and removing workspace members. These mirror the team-management surface in the app.

tags.name: Data Sources
tags.description: Operations for listing, validating, and creating workspace data sources. In this phase the documented connector flow is PostgreSQL-only and remains workspace-scoped and session-authenticated.

tags.name: Queries
tags.description: Operations for browsing, running, saving, renaming, and deleting workspace queries. Query execution is read-only against connected data sources, while query-library mutations remain workspace-scoped and session-authenticated.

tags.name: Visualizations
tags.description: Operations for reading, creating, updating, and removing query-owned visualizations. Visualization state is stored as structured domain data and rendered server-side into ECharts options when needed.

tags.name: Visualization Themes
tags.description: Operations for listing and managing workspace visualization themes plus the built-in system theme. Theme-management writes stay aligned with workspace settings permissions.

current-origin.description: Use the current sqlbook environment origin and example resource ids.

apiUrl.description: Base URL for the current environment.

workspaceId.description: Example workspace id used in code samples.

memberId.description: Example workspace member id used in code samples.

queryId.description: Example saved query id used in code samples.

themeId.description: Example visualization theme id used in code samples.

threadId.description: Example chat thread id used in code samples.

patch.description: Renames the current workspace. This is a low-risk write in chat, but API callers should still expect server-side permission checks. The new name should be sent as a clean target value without surrounding conversational punctuation.

delete.description: Deletes the current workspace. This is a destructive operation and is owner-only. Successful responses may include a redirect path for product surfaces that should return to the workspace index.

patch.description: Renames the current user's active private chat thread in the current workspace. This endpoint can only affect the authenticated user's own thread, even if another thread id is guessed or supplied.

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X PATCH '{{apiUrl}}api/v1/workspaces/{{workspaceId}}/chat-threads/{{threadId}}' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Cookie: _sqlbook_session=<session-cookie>' \
  --data '{"title":"10 longest standing users"}'

get.description: Returns detailed member data for the current workspace. Consumers should expect names, emails, role labels, role ids, and status labels in the response payload.

post.description: Invites a new member into the current workspace. `first_name`, `last_name`, `email`, and `role` are all required.
Resends a pending invitation. Provide either `member_id` or `email`. Only pending invitations can be resent, and resend cooldown rules are enforced server-side.

patch.description: Updates the role for an existing workspace member. This is a high-risk write in chat and remains server-authorized here as well. Role values must be one of the editable non-owner roles allowed by policy.

delete.description: Removes a workspace member. Owners cannot be removed. Accepted members may trigger downstream notification side effects after removal.

get.description: Returns the data sources attached to the current workspace. This read endpoint is available to workspace owners, admins, and users, but not read-only members. The payload is safe to consume directly in chat or UI code because it is already scoped to the current workspace and includes connector type, lifecycle status, selected table counts, and related query counts.

post.description: Creates a PostgreSQL data source after validating the connection details and selected tables. This endpoint is workspace-scoped, session-authenticated, and owner/admin-only.

Tests a PostgreSQL connection without creating the data source. The response includes the tables that are discoverable with the supplied credentials, which makes it suitable for both the create flow and a chat-assisted validation step. This endpoint remains owner/admin-only.

get.description: Returns saved queries in the current workspace query library. Results can optionally be filtered by datasource or a case-insensitive name search. Each saved query remains separate from any thread-local chat query history, but responses may include an optional `chat_source` object when that saved query originated from chat and the current requester can still access the source thread.

post.description: Saves a read-only query into the current workspace query library. Callers must provide SQL and identify the datasource by id or name. If `name` is omitted, the server will ask the model to generate one from the query purpose and recent chat context. Exact duplicates are prevented by a server-owned query fingerprint based on datasource plus normalized SQL: saving the same query again returns the existing saved query with `save_outcome: "already_saved"` instead of creating a duplicate. If that auto-generated name would collide with a different saved query in the same workspace, the server returns a validation error with `error_code: "generated_name_conflict"` plus the proposed name and conflicting query so callers can ask whether to keep that name or choose another. If model-based naming is unavailable, the server returns a validation error with `error_code: "name_generation_failed"` so callers can ask the user what name to use. When a save originated from workspace chat, the saved query may later expose optional `chat_source` provenance to users who can still access that private thread.

saveQuery.summary: Save a query to the library

value.name: User count
value.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM public.users
value.question: How many users do I have?

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST '{{apiUrl}}api/v1/workspaces/{{workspaceId}}/queries' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Cookie: _sqlbook_session=<session-cookie>' \
  --data '{"name":"User count","sql":"SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM public.users","question":"How many users do I have?","data_source_id":31}'

post.description: Runs a read-only query against a connected datasource in the current workspace. Callers can send either a plain-language `question` for SQL generation or direct read-only SQL via `sql`. If multiple datasources or tables plausibly match, the server may return a validation-style clarification prompt instead of guessing.

value.question: How many users do I have?

value.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM public.users

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST '{{apiUrl}}api/v1/workspaces/{{workspaceId}}/queries/run' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Cookie: _sqlbook_session=<session-cookie>' \
  --data '{"question":"How many users do I have?","data_source_id":31}'

patch.description: Updates a saved query in the current workspace query library. Callers can send `name`, `sql`, or both in one request. Rename-only updates still work, but SQL updates now happen in place rather than requiring a save-new-then-delete flow. If the saved query is linked to chat provenance, the thread-local chat query reference is updated as well so older names can still resolve in chat follow-ups. If an SQL update would collide with another saved query fingerprint in the same datasource, the server returns a validation error instead of overwriting or duplicating it.

value.name: User count by super admin status
value.sql: SELECT super_admin, COUNT(*) AS user_count FROM public.users GROUP BY super_admin ORDER BY super_admin

x-codeSamples.label: curl
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X PATCH '{{apiUrl}}api/v1/workspaces/{{workspaceId}}/queries/{{queryId}}' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Cookie: _sqlbook_session=<session-cookie>' \
  --data '{"name":"User count by super admin status","sql":"SELECT super_admin, COUNT(*) AS user_count FROM public.users GROUP BY super_admin ORDER BY super_admin"}'

delete.description: Deletes a saved query from the current workspace query library. This is destructive; callers should treat it like a confirmed delete in product/chat flows and should not assume the query can be recovered. Deletion removes the saved-library record, but any linked thread-local chat query reference remains as private chat history without a `saved_query_id`.

get.description: Returns every saved visualization currently configured for the saved query. Each visualization is query-owned and identified by `chart_type`.
Returns one saved query-owned visualization by `chart_type`, including the structured visualization payload plus preview option payloads for dark and light mode when it exists.

patch.description: Creates or updates the saved query-owned visualization for the requested `chart_type`. Callers send structured visualization state, not raw full ECharts option blobs.

delete.description: Deletes one saved query-owned visualization for the requested `chart_type` without deleting the query itself or affecting other saved visualization types.

get.description: Returns the built-in system theme plus the current workspace's visualization themes.

post.description: Creates a workspace-owned visualization theme with explicit dark and light ECharts theme JSON variants.

get.description: Returns a single visualization theme. The `id` may be a workspace theme id or the system theme reference key `system.default_theming`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Workspace theme id or the system theme reference key.

patch.description: Updates a workspace-owned visualization theme. The system theme is read-only and cannot be updated through this route.

delete.description: Deletes a workspace-owned visualization theme. Default workspace themes must be changed before deletion.

post.description: Duplicates either the built-in system theme or an existing workspace theme into a new editable workspace-owned theme.

patch.description: Marks a workspace-owned visualization theme as the workspace default for new visualizations.

cookieAuth.name: _sqlbook_session
cookieAuth.description: Use the authenticated sqlbook session cookie.

queryId.name: id
queryId.description: The saved query id in the current workspace.

queryVisualizationQueryId.name: query_id
queryVisualizationQueryId.description: The saved query id that owns these visualizations.

chartType.name: chart_type
chartType.description: The visualization type to target for this saved query, for example `line` or `pie`.

threadId.name: id
threadId.description: The current user's chat thread id in the current workspace.

ToolForbidden.description: Action forbidden for current role or workspace scope

value.message: I can't run that action with your current workspace permissions.
Please provide a workspace name.

QuerySaveValidationError.description: Invalid payload for requested query save operation

value.message: Run a query here first, or provide SQL and a data source so I can save it.
I could not generate a saved query name just now. Please tell me what name you want to use.
I can save this as "User count", but there is already a different saved query with that name in the library. Do you want to keep that name or choose another?

conflicting_query.name: User count
conflicting_query.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS user_count FROM public.users WHERE super_admin = true

value.message: There was an unexpected error while running that action.
I renamed that saved query to "List of users".

query.name: List of users
query.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM public.users

value.message: I updated that saved query to "User count by super admin status".

query.name: User count by super admin status
query.sql: SELECT super_admin, COUNT(*) AS user_count FROM public.users GROUP BY super_admin ORDER BY super_admin

value.message: I updated the saved query in the query library: 5 longest standing users.

query.name: 5 longest standing users
query.sql: SELECT id, first_name, last_name, email, created_at FROM public.users ORDER BY created_at ASC NULLS LAST LIMIT 10

value.message: I deleted the saved query "List of users".

VisualizationValidationError.description: Invalid payload for requested visualization operation

value.message: That visualization type is not available.

VisualizationThemeValidationError.description: Invalid payload for requested visualization-theme operation

value.message: Name can't be blank

ToolRateLimited.description: Too many requests. This endpoint is temporarily rate limited.

value.message: You've run too many queries too quickly. Please pause for a moment and try again.

extract_category_values.description: Optional category extraction toggle for the source setup flow.

question.description: Plain-language question for the server to translate into read-only SQL.

sql.description: Direct read-only SQL. Must be a safe SELECT/WITH query.

name.description: Optional saved-query title. If omitted, the server generates one.

question.description: Optional original plain-language question used to help generate a default title.

theme_reference.description: Workspace theme reference or the built-in system theme reference.

reference.description: Theme reference to duplicate, for example `system.default_theming` or `workspace_theme:31`.

message_id.description: Optional source/result message id inside that thread.

path.description: App path back to the source chat thread/message.

suggested_name.description: Optional model-generated suggestion for a clearer saved-query name after a substantive SQL update.

current_name.description: Current saved query name when a rename suggestion is present.

sql.description: Replacement read-only SQL for the saved query.

message.example: You've run too many queries too quickly. Please pause for a moment and try again.
